title: 控方证人
summary:
Following the radio transmission claiming her late husband, Patrick, would visit her on Friday at 9:30 PM, Mary Hart prepares for her death. She writes a note to Dr. Menner to be delivered in the event of her passing and instructs her maid, Elizabeth Marshall, to grant her an extra fifty pounds beyond the fifty-pound bequest in the will. Mary also organizes the distribution of her jewelry and personal belongings to various relatives. She reviews her will, which leaves two five-hundred-pound checks to her sister and eldest nephew, with the remainder of the estate going to her nephew, Charles Richewright.

On Friday evening, Mary sits alone by the fireplace waiting for the appointed time. At 9:30 PM, she hears a rustling and a knock at the door. A silhouette wearing Victorian-era clothing and a beard appears; believing it is Patrick, Mary dies from the shock. Elizabeth finds her an hour later, and although Dr. Menner and Charles are summoned, Mary has already passed away.

Two days later, Dr. Menner reviews Mary's note and concludes she died from the shock of a hallucination. Following this, Charles begins destroying evidence of his involvement by burning a brown beard, hiding Victorian clothing in the attic, and disposing of radio wires. It is revealed that Charles orchestrated the event to secure his inheritance, having planned the "shock" after Dr. Menner previously suggested Mary could have lived many years with proper care. Charles then begins managing the funeral arrangements.
